qse_usage_examples.overview: Complete workflow examples demonstrating how to use the Quantum Safe Explorer mode for different languages and scenarios.

example.name: java_quantum_readiness_full_scan
example.scenario: User wants to perform a complete quantum readiness assessment on a Java project
example.user_request: "Scan this Java project for quantum safety"

step.action: Construct and execute Cryptographic Analytics command
Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: Both Cryptographic Analytics and API Discovery scans completed successfully. QSE reports are available in the output directory.

example.name: go_api_discovery
example.scenario: User wants to scan a Go project for quantum-vulnerable cryptographic APIs
example.user_request: "Check my Go code for quantum safety issues"

step.action: Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: API Discovery scan completed successfully for Go project. QSE report available showing cryptographic API usage.

example.name: python_current_directory
example.scenario: User wants to scan the current Python project directory
example.user_request: "Run quantum safe scan on this Python project"

step.note: No specific path provided, use current workspace

step.action: Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: API Discovery scan completed for Python project. Cryptographic API usage analysis available in QSE report.

example.name: cpp_specific_directory
example.scenario: User wants to scan a specific C++ project directory

step.action: Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: API Discovery scan completed for C++ project at specified path.

example.name: dart_flutter_project
example.scenario: User wants to scan a Flutter/Dart project
example.user_request: "Analyze my Flutter app for quantum safety"

step.action: Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: API Discovery scan completed for Dart/Flutter project.

example.name: java_gradle_project
example.scenario: User wants to scan a Gradle-based Java project with custom build directory
example.user_request: "Run cryptographic analysis on this Gradle project"

step.action: Construct and execute Cryptographic Analytics command
Construct and execute API Discovery command

step.result: Complete quantum readiness assessment completed for Gradle project.

example.name: ambiguous_language_detection
example.scenario: Project contains multiple languages, detection is ambiguous

step.result: Finds both Java and Python files in similar quantities

step.question: This project contains multiple languages. Which language should I scan for quantum safety?

suggestions.suggest: Java (.java) - Supports both Cryptographic Analytics and API Discovery

example.name: no_class_files_found
example.scenario: Java project but no compiled class files found
example.user_request: "Run quantum safe scan on this Java project"

step.result: No target/classes or build/classes directories found

step.action: Use input path as fallback for class file path
step.note: QSE will handle missing class files appropriately

step.action: Execute Cryptographic Analytics with fallback path

request.interpretation: Run appropriate QSE scan(s) for detected language
request.action: Detect language, construct command, execute scan
For Java: both scans; For others: API Discovery
If Java: run with -da flag; Otherwise: explain limitation
Run without -da flag for any supported language

template.name: scan_started
template.message: Starting Quantum Safe Explorer scan for [LANGUAGE] project. Scan type: [SCAN_TYPE] Input path: [PATH]

template.name: java_both_scans
template.message: Running complete quantum readiness assessment for Java project: 1. Cryptographic Analytics scan (analyzing bytecode) 2. API Discovery scan (analyzing source code)

template.name: scan_completed
template.message: Quantum Safe Explorer scan completed successfully. [SCAN_TYPE] analysis finished for [LANGUAGE] project. Results are available in the QSE output directory.

template.name: language_not_supported
template.message: The detected language [LANGUAGE] is not supported by Quantum Safe Explorer. Supported languages: Java, Go, Dart, Python, C++

issue.name: qse_not_found
issue.symptom: Command fails with "No such file or directory"
issue.diagnosis: QSE installation path incorrect or QSE not installed
issue.solution: Verify QSE is installed at /usr/local/bin/qse-cli-artifacts Check if cli.sh script exists and is executable

issue.name: invalid_path
issue.solution: Verify the input path exists using list_files Use absolute paths to avoid ambiguity

issue.name: no_source_files
issue.symptom: Scan completes but finds no files
issue.diagnosis: Wrong language specified or files in unexpected location
issue.solution: Re-run language detection Verify file extensions match specified language Check if source files are in subdirectories

best_practices.practice: Always detect language automatically before asking user
For Java projects, always run both scan types for complete assessment
Use absolute paths when possible to avoid ambiguity
Verify paths exist before constructing commands
Don't attempt to parse or summarize QSE output - let the tool generate its reports
If language detection is uncertain, ask user rather than guessing
For Java, search for class files in standard build directories
Execute commands one at a time and wait for completion
